personality_one_liner: A crisp, high-utility dashboard that feels native, snappy, and effortlessly organized using a warm, earthy palette.

spacing.rule: Generous padding around content to allow breathing room. Ensure hit targets are 44x44. Use md/lg gaps between cards.

do's: Apply to sticky header with bell and 3-dot menu over scrolling content.
Apply to the bottom tab bar.
Apply over hero featured cards when paired with imagery underneath.
dont's: Never blur list rows or form inputs.

screens.name: Navigation Structure
screens.purpose: Provides the structural shell with top header, side drawer, and bottom tabs.

states.loading: Skeleton overlay of header and tabs.

screens.layout: The root is a Drawer Navigator. The Drawer contains Account Settings, Billing, Integrations, Help & Support, and Sign Out. Inside the Drawer sits the Bottom Tab Navigator (Home, Tasks, Analytics, Profile). The Header is globally configured to display a hamburger icon on the left (to open the Drawer), a Title in the center, and a bell icon (with a red notification badge) plus a 3-dot vertical menu icon on the right (which opens a popup with Refresh, Share, Settings). The bottom tabs must remain fixed when navigating through the main tab views.

screens.name: Home (Dashboard Tab)
screens.purpose: Primary landing tab showcasing key metrics, recent activities, and a featured highlight.

states.loading: Skeleton cards mimicking the layout hierarchy.
states.empty: An empty room illustration indicating no current data or activities to show.
states.error: A clean error message card with a retry button using the warning accent.

screens.layout: Vertical scroll. Top section: A Hero feature card with an abstract geometric background, overlaid with a vertical gradient scrim (transparent to surfaceInverse) and white text at the bottom. Below the hero: A 2-column grid of metric cards (e.g., Active Tasks, Pending Reviews). Below that: A 'Recent Activity' list with tinted icon containers for each row.

screens.name: Tasks Tab
screens.purpose: A list view of actionable items.

states.empty: Empty state illustration centered, with 'No pending tasks' and a primary action button.
states.error: Error card with a retry action.

screens.layout: A stacked list of task cards with generous vertical spacing (md gap). Each card has a title, subtitle, and an action chevron. Content scrolls behind the top header and bottom tabs, taking advantage of glassmorphism on elevated surfaces.

screens.name: Analytics Tab
screens.purpose: Visual representation of data over time.

states.empty: Empty state illustration stating 'Not enough data yet'.
states.error: Failed to load chart data error view.

screens.layout: Vertical stack of large, full-width cards containing summary stats and chart placeholders.

screens.name: Profile Tab
screens.purpose: User specific details and account summary.

states.loading: Skeleton layout for avatar and text.

screens.layout: Top section features the user avatar (circular, large) centered over a subtle tinted background, followed by a list of settings rows. Each row has a leading icon inside a tinted brandTertiary square container.

user_avatar.alt: Professional young man with a beard wearing a vest.

instructions_to_main_agent: Strictly follow the color palette provided in this JSON. All blue and purple hues are heavily banned (including indigo, navy, cyan). Use the provided Terracotta/Warm Sand palette for both Light and Dark themes.
The navigation architecture must be a Drawer Navigator wrapping a Bottom Tab Navigator so that the bottom tabs persist on the screen while the user navigates between the main tabs.

The Header (App Bar) should be globally configured across the tabs to include a left hamburger icon, a center title, and right icons (Bell with a red notification count badge, followed by a 3-dot vertical menu icon).
On click of the 3-dot menu, display a clean popup/dropdown menu (e.g., Refresh, Share, Settings) anchored to the icon.
Implement the 'iOS-Native Clean' style: generous spacing (lg/xl padding), clean Plus Jakarta Sans typography, and subtle shadows (tier 1).
Apply Glassmorphism securely on the bottom tab bar and the top sticky header when content scrolls behind them.
For the hero card on the Home Tab, make sure to layer the 'hero_background' image with a top-to-bottom linear gradient scrim (transparent to surfaceInverse) to guarantee text readability.
Use Phosphor icons consistently at weights that match the clean native iOS feel (regular or thin outlines, swapping to filled only for the active state in the bottom tabs).
